raw_consumer_statement:
I am submitting this complaint due to repeated procedural failures by American Express in handling a billing dispute involving a misrepresented travel booking. 

On XX/XX/year>, two charges were posted from the same booking with XXXX XXXX XXXX in the amounts of {$3500.00} and {$660.00}. Both charges were authorized based on the same material misrepresentation at the time of sale : the property was marketed as a XXXX XXXX XXXX hotel, which it was not. 

The booking was marketed, confirmed, and paid for through XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, who acted as the selling agent for the merchant, including processing payment on the merchants behalf using my American Express card. 

American Express reviewed the dispute and ultimately credited the {$3500.00} charge under the dispute category XXXX Not as XXXX. However, despite identical facts, identical documentation, and the same transaction context, American Express repeatedly rebilled the {$660.00} charge. 

The explanation provided for rebilling the smaller charge relied on the merchants assertion that the issue should be resolved with the travel agent, even though the dispute concerns a material misrepresentation at the time of sale and had already been resolved differently for the related charge. 

I repeatedly requested senior-level review, submitted written documentation, uploaded XXXX XXXX XXXX, and made multiple follow-up calls. I was explicitly told that escalation had occurred. Despite this, the dispute continued to be processed througXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  resulting in repeated form responses that did not address the inconsistency or the substance of my requests. 

This complaint is not about a disagreement on the facts. It concerns American Expresss failure to apply a consistent dispute determination to two charges arising from the same misrepresented transaction and its failure to provide the senior-level review that was requested.
